channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: El gobierno de Cuba celebra el legado de Raúl Castro tras la imputación de EE. UU.
item.description: Las autoridades y los medios de comunicación estatales recurrieron a las redes sociales para proyectar la imagen de una nación unida en torno a un anciano estadista venerado.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: El expresidente cubano Raúl Castro, en el centro, en las celebraciones del Primero de Mayo en La Habana a principios de este mes.

item.title: ¿Una Helena de Troya negra? OK. ¿Un Obama blanco? Todavía no.
item.description: Hay buenas razones por las que los actores blancos no deberían interpretar a personajes negros en nuestro momento.
item.dc:creator: John McWhorter

item.title: Keeping Black Brooklyn Alive, One Drink at a Time
item.description: As the departure of New York’s Black residents changes neighborhood culture, one gathering paid tribute to one of its remaining Black institutions, the Tip-Top Bar and Grill.
item.dc:creator: Maya King
item.category: Center for Real Estate and Urban Policy

media:content.media:credit: Elias Williams for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: For one night, the Tip-Top Bar and Grill attracted a mostly Black crowd, a callback to the establishment’s role in the Bedford-Stuyvesant section of Brooklyn.

item.title: How Stars From ‘The Morning Show’ and ‘The League’ Keep Their Love Alive
item.description: Mark Duplass and his wife, Katie Aselton, make movies together, but they also make time to cuddle on the subway.
item.dc:creator: Addie Morfoot

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 23, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: El mercado de la salud vaginal crece cada vez más
item.description: Los vacíos en el conocimiento y cuidado de la salud femenina han generado una oportunidad de negocio. ¿Qué puede salir mal?
item.dc:creator: Carmen Rising

item.title: Xi Calls for All-Out Rescue After Coal Mine Explosion Kills at Least 90 in China
item.description: The death count rose drastically on Saturday as the Chinese leader, Xi Jinping, called for an investigation and emphasized the need to “hold those responsible to account.”
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson

media:content.media:description: Rescuers at the site of a deadly explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in China’s Shanxi Province, on Saturday.

item.title: Cuba merece una nueva historia nacional
item.description: ¿Cómo pueden avanzar los cubanos si no están dispuestos a asumir su pasado?
item.dc:creator: Michael J. Bustamante

media:content.media:description: Imágenes de Raúl Castro adornan las paredes de La Habana, Cuba.

item.title: Australia’s ‘Prettiest Street’ Is Grappling With TikTok and Instagram Fame
item.description: For many years, Tasman Drive in Gerringong was a secret held by locals. Now residents must coexist with tourists hungry for the perfect shot.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung and Matthew Abbott

media:content.media:description: Mayor Cameron McDonald, who oversees Gerringong and its surrounding towns, said the popularity of Tasman Drive had caught the authorities off guard. But he said they were working to address residents’ concerns.

item.title: ‘Amazon.com of South Korea’ Is New Source of Tension With U.S.
item.description: A corporate compliance dispute between South Korea’s government and the online retailer Coupang is testing the resilience of U.S.-South Korean ties.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun and Aaron Krolik

media:content.media:description: President Trump and the president of South Korea, Lee Jae Myung, last year in Gyeongju, South Korea.

item.title: Their Phones Were Stolen in London. Then the Threats Started.
item.description: Tens of thousands of smartphones were reported stolen in the British capital in recent years. For some victims, losing their phone was only the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden and Amelia Nierenberg

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Alex Pikula at home in Chicago. He had his phone stolen while on a trip to London in May 2024, after which his mother started receiving strange and increasingly threatening texts.

item.title: Once Trump’s Co-Pilot Against Iran, Netanyahu Is Now a Mere Passenger
item.description: A partner in the war, Israel has been largely left out of the peace talks, a humbling setback for its prime minister with significant risks for the country.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel with President Trump at Mar-a-Lago in December.

item.title: The Risks of Iran’s Threat to Control the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran is trying to assert its control over the strait by charging for passage. Experts say it is unlikely to happen, but the threat has unsettled the shipping industry.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Ships anchored in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this month. Vessels and workers have been stranded in the Persian Gulf for nearly three months.

item.title: Corrections: May 23, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, May 23, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Kyle Busch, NASCAR’s Often Unbeatable Bad Boy, Dies at 41
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, May 23, 2026

item.title: How a Chemical Tank in Southern California Came to the Brink of Disaster
item.description: Firefighters are left without any options to prevent the tank, filled with a toxic chemical, from collapsing or exploding. The authorities have urged thousands of people to evacuate their homes.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:description: A blocked-off road in Garden Grove, Calif., on Friday.

item.title: HUD Moves to Limit Assistance Animals for Disabled Tenants
item.description: Housing officials were told to exclude emotional support animals and tighten the definition of a service animal when granting accommodations for disabled tenants.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Debra Kamin

media:content.media:description: A service dog at the 2023 Veterans Day parade in Nashville.

item.title: Choice for the Undecided
item.description: Kameron Austin Collins’s latest themeless puzzle: hilarious or nefarious? Why not both?
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: Arrrrrrf, matey! Avast, lest me breath send ye to a watery grave!

item.title: Garden Grove Is a Suburban City Near Theme Parks
item.description: The city is home to part of Little Saigon, the cultural heart of the Vietnamese American population in California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:description: Garden Grove has a large Vietnamese American population and is known for sharing Little Saigon with the neighboring city of Westminster.

item.title: Trump Weighs His Options in Carrying Out New Strikes in Iran
item.description: There is no shortage of targets if he decides to strike: Energy facilities left untouched, the deep underground nuclear storage site at Isfahan and missile sites that appear to have been dug out.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Eric Schmitt, Tyler Pager, Jonathan Swan and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump’s announcement on Friday that he was skipping the wedding this weekend of his son Donald Trump Jr. came as negotiations with Iran over its nuclear program and its blockage of the Strait of Hormuz appeared to have hit a dead end.

item.title: Trump Visits New York to Boost House Republican’s Re-election Hopes
item.description: The visit by President Trump was meant to promote Representative Mike Lawler’s bid to defend his swing seat in New York, but it had the earmarks of a Trump rally.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump appeared at his first rally in New York since his 2024 election to campaign for Representative Mike Lawler.

item.title: Mob Burns Congo Ebola Center Amid Rare Strain Outbreak
item.description: Several hundred people massed at the gates of a hospital to demand the body of a suspected Ebola victim. Violence broke out when staff refused the request.
item.dc:creator: Declan Walsh and Arlette Bashizi

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times

item.title: What is GKN Aerospace, the Company Whose California Plant Is at Risk of Exploding?
item.description: A chemical leak occurred at one of the company’s manufacturing plants in California, raising concerns about an explosion.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra and Niraj Chokshi

media:content.media:description: Water is sprayed on a chemical tank at GKN Aerospace.

item.title: Muere Kyle Busch, piloto bicampeón de NASCAR
item.description: Busch murió tras ser hospitalizado por una “enfermedad grave”, según dijeron en un comunicado conjunto su familia, su escudería y la NASCAR. No se detalló ninguna causa concreta.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman
item.category: National Assn of Stock Car Auto Racing

media:content.media:description: Kyle Busch en febrero. “Hemos perdido a nuestro Kobe Bryant”, publicó en Instagram el piloto Denny Hamlin, antiguo compañero de equipo de Busch.

item.title: What Is Methyl Methacrylate, the Chemical at the Center of the Garden Grove Crisis?
item.description: The E.P.A. warns that short-term exposure to the compound can cause skin and eye irritation and respiratory issues.
item.dc:creator: Emily Baumgaertner Nunn

media:content.media:description: Methyl methacrylate is widely used to manufacture resins and acrylic plastics. It can be found in products like advertising signs, light fixtures, plumbing and latex paint.

item.title: Meth and Fentanyl Found in Home Where Emergency Responders Fell Ill
item.description: More than a dozen emergency workers were treated after responding to a report of an overdose at a New Mexico home.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:description: The authorities in New Mexico identified the substances that killed three people and hospitalized more than a dozen emergency responders on Wednesday.

item.title: Rob Base, Rapper Known for ‘It Takes Two,’ Dies at 59
item.description: The singer died on Friday after a “private battle with cancer,” according to his official Instagram account.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:description: The rapper Rob Base in 2022. He was known for the hit song “It Takes Two.”

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard Resigns as Intelligence Chief
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, announced on social media on Friday that she would be leaving her post to care for her husband, who is battling an “extremely rare form of bone cancer.”
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard at a House hearing in March. She said on Friday that she would remain in her post as the nation’s intelligence chief until June 30.

item.title: Quienes soliciten la ‘green card’ deberán hacerlo fuera de EE. UU.
item.description: Es probable que el cambio afecte a cientos de miles de personas. También podría provocar más separaciones familiares mientras las parejas o los parientes esperan la decisión sobre la solicitud.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Albert Sun

media:content.media:credit: Libby March para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Existen varias vías para que los extranjeros obtengan la tarjeta verde, que les permite vivir y trabajar en Estados Unidos como residentes permanentes.

item.title: U.S. to Block Entry to More Noncitizens Who May Have Been Exposed to Ebola
item.description: The Trump administration announced plans to expand a ban on entering the United States to legal permanent residents who had been in the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda or South Sudan.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli and Hamed Aleaziz
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Health care workers check temperatures as people pass through a checkpoint in Goma, Democratic Republic of Congo, on Thursday.

item.title: Lawsuit Argues Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund Excludes Those He Targeted
item.description: The constellation of individuals and groups involved claims to have suffered partisan attacks by the federal government under Trump, yet would not be compensated.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rioters during the attack at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: Deadly Explosions Injure Dozens of Firefighters on Staten Island Barge
item.description: Two explosions rocked a barge on the North Shore of Staten Island, injuring 34 people. The resulting fire was under control, but was still burning.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag and Nicol León Arge

item.title: A Ford Truck, Home to Newborn Robins, Is Stuck at a Kansas Car Dealer
item.description: Employees of a dealership in Olathe, Kan., found the nest, which is protected under federal law, on top of one of the truck’s tires.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: Sports Utility Vehicles and Light Trucks

item.title: Toxic Explosion Feared at California Chemical Plant
item.description: Evacuation orders were issued to 40,000 Orange County residents as firefighters tried to prevent a spill or an explosion. Health officials said they couldn’t predict the impact of a toxic release.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Carlo Petrini, Whose Slow Food Movement Transformed How We Eat, Dies at 76
item.description: Through his Slow Food organization, he spurred a worldwide shift toward sustainability and thoughtful cuisine.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

media:content.media:description: Carlo Petrini in 2012. He urged people at all points in the supply chain to embrace food that he described as “good, clean and fair.”

item.title: In Dissecting 2024 Loss, Democrats’ Report Ignores Gaza, Biden’s Age
item.description: The nearly 200-page draft autopsy indicated that the party had failed to fully grapple with why Kamala Harris had lost to Donald J. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer and Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kamala Harris speaks at the Democratic National Convention in Chicago in 2024.

item.title: Europeans Remain Wary as Trump Promises to Deploy Troops to Poland
item.description: President Trump has promised to deploy 5,000 U.S. troops to Poland, seemingly reversing course from his previous statements. NATO allies responded cautiously during a summit on Friday and pushed for greater military self-reliance.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: U.S. Army soldiers before a live-fire exercise in Drawsko Pomorskie, Poland, last year.

item.title: White House Approves $9 Billion for Spy Agencies to Catch Up on A.I.
item.description: The C.I.A. and N.S.A. cannot fully deploy the latest models on their classified systems because of a shortage of cutting-edge chips.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz and Julian E. Barnes

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jensen Huang, president and CEO of NVIDIA, gives a presentation in Washington last October.

item.title: Waymo Pauses Service in 6 Cities After Cars Drive Into Floodwaters
item.description: Waymo has paused its driverless taxi service in six cities after video emerged showing an empty car stuck on a flooded road in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Waymo also announced it was temporarily halting its highway service this week to improve its navigation system.

item.title: Trump’s Intelligence Chief Resigns
item.description: Also, mediators rush to save the U.S.-Iran cease-fire. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: Warsh Is Sworn in as Fed Chair
item.description: Kevin M. Warsh, President Trump’s pick for Fed chair, said he would lead a “reform-oriented” Federal Reserve in his first remarks after being sworn in.
item.dc:creator: Christina Kelso

media:content.media:description: Kevin M. Warsh was sworn in as Federal Reserve chair during a ceremony with President Trump on Friday at the White House.

item.title: The D.N.C. Chairman, Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund and More Politics News
item.description: He has focused on internal politics, building a loyal base despite tactical disputes.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ken Martin has fulfilled a campaign promise to dispense more funds to each of the D.N.C.’s state parties.

item.title: Trump Administration Offers Conflicting View of Civil Rights Cuts
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon has said she wants to hire more civil rights lawyers, but the White House has proposed cutting that office in half.
item.dc:creator: Michael C. Bender

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In March of last year, Ms. McMahon fired about half of her department’s civil rights office. In December, she gave up on the firings in the face of mounting legal challenges and an expanding backlog of discrimination complaints in schools.

item.title: Maryland Democrats Plan to Eliminate State’s Lone Republican Seat in Time for 2028
item.description: A top state lawmaker who opposed drawing new districts for the fall election now says he supports a constitutional amendment that could pave the way to an all-Democrat map for the next cycle.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti

media:content.media:description: Bill Ferguson, left, the Democratic leader of the Maryland Senate, has been at odds with Gov. Wes Moore, a Democrat, over whether to redraw the state’s congressional boundaries this year.

item.title: G.O.P. Break With Trump Reflects Limits of Party’s Post-Jan. 6 Truce
item.description: Republicans, seeing President Trump’s personal agenda diverging from their political interests, vented their outrage about paying those who threatened their lives on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:description: Senator Bill Cassidy, Republican of Louisiana, at the Capitol this week after losing his primary to a Trump-backed challenger.

item.title: Green Card Seekers Must Leave U.S. to Apply, Trump Administration Says
item.description: The change is likely to affect hundreds of thousands of people. It could also lead to more family separations as spouses or relatives wait for application decisions, immigration lawyers said.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Albert Sun

media:content.media:credit: Libby March for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: There are various pathways for foreigners to obtain green cards, which grant them the ability to live and work in the United States as permanent residents.

item.title: Colbert’s Final ‘Late Show’ Draws 6.7 Million Viewers
item.description: The total tripled his usual audience this season. But it is only about half of what Jay Leno and David Letterman attracted for their final episodes.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Waymo Suspends Service in Six Cities After Cars Drove Into Flooded Roads
item.description: The temporary shutdowns came after videos emerged showing two Waymo cars stopped on swamped streets in Atlanta on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson

media:content.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Waymo also announced it was temporarily halting its highway service this week to improve its navigation system.

item.title: Judge Dismisses Criminal Case Against Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia
item.description: The move deals an embarrassing blow to the Trump administration, which made the Maryland man, Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, the face of its deportation campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia outside a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement in Baltimore last year.
